meta.methodology: API-level exhaustive + static source audit (no Playwright MCP browser tools available in session)

issues.element: Advanced sheet → Textarea HEARTBEAT.md + Save button (or direct PUT /api/heartbeat)
issues.title: PUT /api/heartbeat with empty string destroys HEARTBEAT.md
issues.description: Sending {content:""} to PUT /api/heartbeat writes zero bytes to /root/.forgeclaw/HEARTBEAT.md. The toast reports 'HEARTBEAT.md saved' and the server returns {success:true,lines:1,source:'core'}. All file-origin cron jobs will disappear on next fs.watch hot-reload. This was actually triggered during testing and the file had to be reconstructed from screenshot evidence.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Edit packages/dashboard/src/app/api/heartbeat/route.ts PUT handler. Add: if (typeof content !== 'string' || content.trim().length < 10) return 400. Also core.writeHeartbeat should write-tmp-then-rename and create a .bak. Frontend: handleSaveHeartbeat in crons-tab.tsx should show a confirm dialog when heartbeat.trim() === '' before calling PUT.

issues.element: Form sheet Save button (POST /api/crons) and PUT action=update
issues.title: Backend accepts arbitrary invalid cron expression
issues.description: POST /api/crons with schedule:'foo bar baz' (or any garbage string) returns success:true and persists. The cron engine will throw on parse every run. Frontend validateSchedule blocks this via canSave, but the backend has NO validation so any direct API call / retry / automation can insert a broken cron.
issues.diagnosis_hints: In packages/dashboard/src/app/api/crons/route.ts POST handler, import CronExpressionParser from 'cron-parser' and call parser.parse(schedule.trim()) inside try/catch, return 400 on failure. Same for PUT action='update'.

issues.element: Pause / Resume button on file-origin cron cards
issues.title: action=toggle on file-origin jobs succeeds without origin guard
issues.description: The PUT handler's action==='toggle' branch (route.ts lines 140-150) has no origin check, unlike action==='update' which correctly returns 403 for file-origin. Toggling a file-origin cron updates the SQLite row, but on next HEARTBEAT.md fs.watch reload the file-origin cron is re-parsed and the pause is lost. State drift.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Add: if (core.getCronJob(id)?.origin === 'file') return 403 inside the toggle branch. Also see FRONTEND-006.

issues.title: Pause/Resume button not disabled for file-origin cards
issues.description: Edit (line 154) and Delete (line 173) buttons correctly set disabled={isFileOrigin} with title tooltip. The Pause/Resume button does NOT — so users can click it, see an optimistic state change, and think it worked. Combined with BACKEND-001 this lets a bad state persist until next fs.watch reload.
issues.diagnosis_hints: In cron-card.tsx, add disabled={isFileOrigin} and title={isFileOrigin ? fileTooltip : (job.enabled ? 'Pause this cron' : 'Resume this cron')} to the toggle Button. Remove if you decide db-level toggle of file-origin is intentional — in which case document it in the tooltip.

issues.title: POST accepts empty name
issues.description: POST with name:'' returns success:true. Card renders with empty title — user cannot identify it to delete via UI. Frontend canSave blocks but backend doesn't.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Add name.trim().length validation to POST + PUT action=update in route.ts.

issues.title: POST accepts empty prompt
issues.description: POST with prompt:'' returns success:true. Empty-prompt cron has nothing to execute. Frontend blocks but backend doesn't.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Same place as BACKEND-003; add prompt.trim().length > 0 check.

issues.element: Form Save in edit mode (PUT action=update)
issues.title: PUT action=update on nonexistent id returns success:true
issues.description: When core.updateCronJob returns falsy for a nonexistent id, the route falls through to the catch-all {success:true,source:'mock'} on line 190. Indistinguishable from a real update. Silent data loss if the id is wrong.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Check updated truthiness before returning success; return 404 when core returns falsy. Also consider removing the broad catch-all at line 190.

issues.title: PUT action=toggle on nonexistent id returns success:true
issues.description: Same root cause as BACKEND-009 — final catch-all returns success regardless.

issues.title: POST with empty body returns success:true with fake timestamp id
issues.description: POST /api/crons with {} returns {success:true,job:{id:1775910853083,origin:'db'},source:'mock'}. The core.createCronJob failed (NOT NULL violation on required columns) but the route falls through to Date.now() mock id. Frontend handleSubmit cannot distinguish from real success; it shows 'Cron created' toast and refetches — but the job doesn't exist.
issues.reproduction: curl -X POST /api/crons -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' → success with fake id 1775910853083
issues.diagnosis_hints: Remove the mock-fallback branch in POST (lines 71-80). Validate required fields at the top and return 400.

issues.element: Form Save (POST /api/crons) — accepts origin from body
issues.title: POST accepts origin:'file' from request body, creating undeletable jobs
issues.description: POST with {origin:'file',sourceFile:'/tmp/fake.md',...} creates a file-origin cron via the dashboard API. DELETE then refuses with 'Cannot delete file-origin jobs'. The cron becomes permanent until direct SQLite cleanup. This is how the DCRV-TEST-as-file pollution from the visual testing phase was produced.

evidence.proof_of_undeletability: DELETE id=29 returned {success:false,error:'Cannot delete file-origin jobs from dashboard'}
evidence.cleanup_required: direct better-sqlite3 DELETE FROM cron_jobs WHERE id=29

issues.diagnosis_hints: Remove origin and sourceFile from the destructured body in route.ts POST. Hard-code: origin:'db', sourceFile:null. File-origin crons must only be created by the HEARTBEAT.md parser in the core process, never via HTTP.

issues.title: POST with schedule:null or prompt:null returns fake mock id
issues.description: Sending null values triggers NOT NULL constraint in core.createCronJob which returns null; route falls through to Date.now() mock id. Fake success again.
issues.reproduction: POST with {schedule:null,prompt:null,...} → {success:true, job:{id:1775910809471,...},source:'mock'}; id not in subsequent GET

issues.title: handleToggle has no failure handling and no rollback
issues.description: The handler does an optimistic setJobs, then fire-and-forget fetch. No res.ok check, no state rollback on failure, no toast. If the server returns 500 or the network drops, the UI shows a state that doesn't match the DB until the next fetchJobs (which only runs on save/delete/heartbeat-save, NOT on toggle).
issues.diagnosis_hints: Wrap fetch in try/catch/res.ok, and on failure setJobs back (invert enabled) + showToast('Toggle failed','error'). See BACKEND-001/010 which amplify this on the error side.

issues.title: No length limit on name or prompt
issues.description: POST with 512-char name persists. Card CardTitle has no 'truncate' class, so visual breakage is guaranteed for long names. No input maxLength on the frontend either.
issues.reproduction: POST with name of 512 A characters → persisted at full length
issues.diagnosis_hints: Add maxLength on Input in cron-form-sheet.tsx AND server-side cap (suggest name <=100, prompt <=5000) in route.ts.

issues.title: PUT with unknown action returns success:true
issues.description: PUT with action:'explode' returns {success:true,action:'explode',source:'mock'}. Silently swallows typos and forward-compat attempts.
issues.diagnosis_hints: After known actions (toggle/update/run_now), return 400 'Unknown action' instead of the mock fallback.

issues.title: PUT with missing action returns success:true
issues.diagnosis_hints: Validate action at top of PUT handler.

issues.title: PUT /api/heartbeat without content leaks JS error
issues.description: PUT with {} returns {success:false, error:"Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'split')"}. Exposes internal implementation detail. Fix is a one-line typeof guard.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Top of PUT: if (typeof content !== 'string') return 400 'content must be a string'.

issues.title: PUT /api/heartbeat accepts unbounded payload
issues.description: 1MB payload of 'x' accepted and written to HEARTBEAT.md. No size cap. Disk-fill vector.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Check content.length <= 64*1024 in PUT handler (or whatever sensible cap).

issues.title: Run now has no toast on success path
issues.description: handleRunNow only shows a toast on !data.success. Today 100% of calls fail (known limitation — bot process required), so a toast always fires. But the success branch is empty. Once the bot-side fix is wired, success will be silent and scrolling will lose the visual feedback. Matches VIS-014.
issues.diagnosis_hints: In handleRunNow, show an immediate showToast('Triggering cron...') on click regardless of server response. Or show toast in the else-branch after data.success.

issues.title: GET /api/crons/0/logs returns empty instead of error
issues.description: id=0 passes isNaN check and returns {logs:[],source:'core'} — indistinguishable from 'valid job with no logs'. Minor.
issues.diagnosis_hints: Add if (jobId <= 0) return 400 to the logs route.
